zotero.abstractNote: Performativity describes settings where a model's predictions influence the future data distribution. Estimating the causal effect of a model on the population is a central challenge in this context. Existing approaches typically rely on randomized experiments, which are often infeasible or unethical. We propose a framework for estimating performativity without treatment randomization. Our approach leverages the structure of the decision-making process to identify causal effects from observational data. We provide theoretical guarantees for our estimators and demonstrate their effectiveness on synthetic and real-world datasets.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 41st International Conference on Machine Learning
